title: Miktiss
content:

'''Miktiss''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Saurton]] [[Soldier/Legends|warrior]] from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Essowyn]] in the [[Trax sector/Legends|Trax sector]]. A loyal supporter of the ''[[Des'mar]]'', a faction of Saurtons who supported the [[Mining/Legends|mining]] companies that dominated their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]], Miktiss participated in military operations against the ''[[Quenno]]'', a group opposed to the presence of the corporations. However, since his actions were illegal, he was eventually brought before the [[Council of Elders (Saurton)|Council of Elders]] to answer for his crimes. Despite being employed by members of the Council who were ''Des'mar'' supporters themselves, he was sentenced to [[Death/Legends|death]] since the proof of his guilt was absolute. However, his loyalty to the ''Des'mar'' cause was so strong that he refused to implicate members of the Council in his actions. As a reward for his sacrifice, he was [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggled]] off-planet prior to his execution on the proviso that he never returned to Essowyn.

Striking out toward the [[Kathol sector/Legends|Kathol sector]], Miktiss turned to [[Pirate/Legends|piracy]] and formed a group known as the [[Des'maric Pirates]] around [[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]]. Stealing several [[GAT-12 Skipray Blastboat|Skipray Blastboats]] and an [[Action IV transport]] to use as the basis of his pirate [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]], he entered into an arrangement with a local [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]], [[Crev Bombaasa/Legends|Crev Bombaasa]], who supplied him with [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] travel routes in exchange for a share of the pirates' take. Miktiss used his small [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] to capture several [[Starship/Legends|vessels]] and intimidate primitive backwater planets throughout the Kathol [[Sector/Legends|sector]].

==Biography==
===Early life===
The [[Saurton]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] Miktiss was [[Reproduction/Legends|born]] on his [[Species/Legends|species]]' [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of [[Essowyn]],<ref name="TDC" /> located within the [[Trax sector/Legends|Trax sector]] of the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]].<ref name="TEA-OC" /> The [[Planet/Legends|planet]] was controlled by various [[Mining/Legends|mining]] companies, who were aided by a coalition of Saurton groups known as the ''[[Des'mar]]'' to maintain their power base.<ref name="TDC" /> The ''Des'mar'' believed that the presence of the mining companies was vital to Essowyn's growth, as they brought in outside trade and culture.<ref name="POG1">''[[Planets of the Galaxy, Volume One]]''</ref> Miktiss served as a [[Soldier/Legends|warrior]] for the ''Des'mar'', and worked to disrupt the ''[[Quenno]]'',<ref name="TDC" /> a faction of Saurtons who sought to remove the mining companies from Essowyn, as they believed that control of the planet's resources should lie with the [[Council of Elders (Saurton)|Council of Elders]], the traditional rulers of the planet.<ref name="POG1" /> Miktiss personally led several assaults on protests organized by the ''Quenno''. However, Miktiss's actions were illegal, and incontrovertible proof of his crimes was presented to the Saurton Council of Elders. Although several of the Council were ''Des'mar'' supporters themselves, and had even employed Miktiss, they were bound by their laws to [[Death/Legends|execute]] the warrior for his crimes. Despite the fact that the Council had proclaimed sentence on him, Miktiss was loyal to the ''Des'mar'' cause and refused to betray them, maintaining that he had acted on his own. His loyalty was rewarded when—prior to his execution—he was secretly [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggled]] off-world on the understanding that he could never set [[Limb/Legends|foot]] on Essowyn again.<ref name="TDC" />

===A life of piracy===
Effectively exiled from his homeworld, Miktiss took to [[Pirate/Legends|piracy]] and forged an organization<ref name="TDC" /> around [[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]],<ref name="FormationDate">The events of ''[[The DarkStryder Campaign]]'' take place in [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]] according to ''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]''. The [[Des'maric Pirates]] began operating in the [[Kathol sector/Legends|Kathol sector]] a [[Standard year/Legends|year]] prior to the events of ''The DarkStryder Campaign'', giving a date of [[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]].</ref> naming them the [[Des'maric Pirates]]. Learning of a trio of [[GAT-12 Skipray Blastboat]]s located at the [[Spaceport/Legends|starport]] on the planet [[Elshandruu Pica]], Miktiss arranged for members of his organization to infiltrate the general vicinity. The Blastboats were being held for sale to [[Kina Margath]],<ref name="TDC" /> owner of [[Margath's on Elshandruu Pica|Margath's]] chain of entertainment establishments who served as a [[Foster Agent]] for the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].<ref name="GG9">''[[Galaxy Guide 9: Fragments from the Rim]]''</ref> While a minor criminal in his employ distracted starport security, Miktiss led the operation to commandeer the [[Starship/Legends|vessels]] and managed to get them off-planet and into [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] before the [[Star system/Legends|system's]] [[Patrol ship/Legends|patrol vessels]] could respond. The Blastboats were gaudily painted and named ''[[Des'maric One]]'', ''[[Des'maric Two]]'', and ''[[Des'maric Three]]'', and Miktiss entrusted command of the vessels to three of his top [[Lieutenant/Legends|lieutenants]]—fellow Saurton and longtime friend [[Ginzet]], a [[Human/Legends|Human]] named [[Jerri]], and an [[Aqualish/Legends|Aqualish]] [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]] called [[Yun (Aqualish)|Yun]].<ref name="TDC" />

[[File:Skipray_blastboat_landing_config.jpg|thumb|left|230px|One of Miktiss's Skipray Blastboats]]
Shortly after obtaining the Blastboats, the pirates captured the [[Corellian Engineering Corporation/Legends|Corellian Engineering Corporation]] [[Action IV transport]] [[Asagov Raider|''Cekbar Servant'']], a [[Bulk freighter/Legends|bulk freighter]] that was registered to a [[Consortium of Nalroni merchants|consortium]] of [[Nalroni/Legends|Nalroni]] [[Merchant/Legends|merchants]] based out of [[Celanon/Legends|Celanon]]. Miktiss renamed the vessel as the ''Asagov Raider'', and used a combination of blackmail and bargaining to force a former [[Sienar Fleet Systems/Legends|Sienar Fleet Systems]] [[Unidentified ship engineer|engineer]] to extensively modify the vessel, including adding extra weapons and [[Armor/Legends|armor]], as well as stronger [[Deflector shield/Legends|shields]], and increasing the ship's speed. Using the ''Asagov Raider'' as a mobile command base, Miktiss moved the pirates to the [[Kathol sector/Legends|Kathol sector]]. He entered into an arrangement with [[Crev Bombaasa/Legends|Crev Bombaasa]],<ref name="TDC" /> a [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] based on the planet [[Pembric II]].<ref name="C">{{DarkStryder|adventure=Crisis (adventure)|book=The DarkStryder Campaign}}</ref> Bombaasa supplied Miktiss with [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] travel routes in exchange for a third of the pirates' loot. Although the pirates' operations were relatively minor—capturing only three bulk freighters<ref name="TDC" /> by [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]]<ref name="Date">The events of ''[[The DarkStryder Campaign]]'' take place in [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]] according to ''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]''.</ref>—Miktiss also used his small [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] to intimidate primitive worlds into paying him tribute.<ref name="TDC" />

The Saurton gathered a group of around one hundred individuals to form the pirates, many of whom were common criminals who would have gladly turned Miktiss in to the [[Law enforcement agency/Legends|authorities]] if they thought it would save themselves. While aware that they had little in the way of real talents, Miktiss nevertheless assigned the most skilled members of his organization to the Blastboats, reasoning that he needed his best people in the vessels that would see actual combat. The group avoided the attention of [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] authorities as the vessels that they had captured were recorded as lost due to hyperspace accidents, and presented a considerable danger to any lightly armed merchant ship traveling through the [[Sector/Legends|sector]]. Miktiss's main tactic was to maneuver a large [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroid]] into the flight path of a target, forcing the vessel out of hyperspace. Before the vessel had a chance to recalculate the hyperspace jump, the Blastboats would move in and surround the vessel, calling for its surrender. Sometimes, the crew of the vessel was granted [[Transport/Legends|transport]] to a habitable planet, although not all crews enjoyed that luxury. Goods seized from the raids were usually sold on Pembric II, or at the [[Tanquilla Beach/Legends|''Tanquilla Beach'']] [[Shadowport/Legends|shadowport]]. Unknown to Miktiss, Bombaasa was secretly planning to take over the Des'maric Pirates, buying the loyalty of several members of the organization, including Ginzet, the being Miktiss regarded as his most trusted lieutenant.<ref name="TDC" />

==Personality and traits==
Miktiss, a Saurton with [[Color/Legends|yellow-green]] coloration, was extremely loyal to the ''Des'mar'' and his people, truly believing that Essowyn needed the mining corporations to prosper. He had no compunctions about employing violence against the ''Quenno'' opposition to ensure the supremacy of the ''Des'mar''. Even when faced with certain death, Miktiss remained loyal to his ideals and refused to blame the Council that had condemned him. As a Saurton, Miktiss was resistant to disease. He was also ruthless, and it was well known that he would space any member of the organization if they were revealed to be a traitor to him.<ref name="TDC" />

A capable warrior, Miktiss usually led from the front, personally overseeing operations to crush ''Quenno'' protests. As his role and responsibilities changed when he formed the Des'maric Pirates, Miktiss did not involve himself in front-line actions, preferring to remain on the ''Asagov Raider'' during raids. He did, however, enjoy personally interrogating prisoners that the pirates captured in the detention cells of the ''Asagov Raider''.<ref name="TDC" />

==Skill and abilities==
Miktiss was a [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] fully capable of flying both space transports and [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]], and could [[Astrogation/Legends|plot courses]] through hyperspace. He was also skilled in the programming of [[Droid/Legends|droids]], and the repair of starfighters. His position as leader of the Des'Maric Pirates gave Miktiss a working knowledge of the planetary systems within the Kathol sector.<ref name="TDC" />

==Equipment==
Miktiss favored using a [[Vibro-ax/Legends|vibro-ax]] and a [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistol]] in battle, and wore a [[Blast vest/Legends|blast vest]] and carried a [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]] in addition to his weapons.<ref name="TDC" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:TheDarkStryderCampaign.jpg|thumb|right|The cover of ''The DarkStryder Campaign''.]]
Miktiss was created for [[West End Games]]' ''[[The DarkStryder Campaign]]'',<ref name="TDC" /> published on [[July 17]], [[1995]].<ref name="BN">{{BN|url=w/star-wars-the-roleplaying-game-box-set/1018175954|text=Star Wars the Roleplaying Game: Darkstryder Campaign Boxed Set}}</ref> Miktiss only appeared in the "Rogues Gallery" section of the book, written by [[Bill Smith]]. While Miktiss did not appear in any of the adventures, he—along with the Des'maric Pirates—were included in the book to give the Gamemaster the option to use him in an original adventure.<ref name="TDC" />
